DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-02-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
CROSS-DEFENDANT CBS OUTDOOR INC.'S DEMURRER TO, OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, MOTION TO STRIKE, THE FIRST AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT.

APPEARANCES: Ali A. Karaouni, Esq. of Miller Starr Regalia 925/935-9400 for pltf/x-deft & moving party; Cindy Lee, Esq. of Law Offices of Cindy Lee 885-5800 for deft/x-complainant & opposing party Mark Rutherford, Trustee of the Shizuo Holdings Trust.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - DEMURRER OVERRULED AS TO 5TH CAUSE OF ACTION. DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO 6TH CAUSE OF ACTION (FRAUD) FOR PLAINTIFF TO PLEAD ACTUAL AND JUSTIFIABLE RELIANCE. MOTION TO STRIKE DENIED. DAMAGES WILL BE SUBJECT TO PROOF. Order signed in open court. (302/CWW) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-07-2010 Continued To Oct-29-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 212

Cross Defendant Cbs Outdoor, Inc'S Demurrer To Cross Complaint
Off Calendar Aug-09-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: CROSS-DEFENDANT CBS OUTDOOR, INC.'S DEMURRER TO CROSS-COMPLAINT. Appearances: Cindy Lee of the Law Offices of Cindy Lee (885.5800) for cross complainant and responding party Mark Rutherford; George Speir of Miller Starr Regalia (925.935.9400) for cross defendant and moving party CBS Outdoor, Inc.

Argued and the Court modified its ruling as follows: As a preliminary matter, the court does not reach the factual issue of who "owns" the signs in this demurrer. Demurrer sustained with 10 days leave to amend as to 5th cause of action (unfair business practices) for cross-complainant to plead fraud with particularity, or to identify the statutory scheme violated, or other unfair conduct. Demurrer sustained with leave to amend as to 6th cause of action for fraud and misrepresentation to plead representations made to cross-complainant, knowledge, intent and justifiable reliance. Demurrer overruled as to remaining causes of action. Further, the Court grants leave to amend the Breach of Contract cause of action to set forth with greater particularity what parts of the contract were breached. Ms. Lee to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797

Cross Defendant Cbs Outdoor, Inc'S Motion To Strike
Off Calendar Aug-09-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: CROSS-DEFENDANT CBS OUTDOOR, INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE. Appearances: Cindy Lee of the Law Offices of Cindy Lee (885.5800) for cross complainant and responding party Mark Rutherford; George Speir of Miller Starr Regalia (925.935.9400) for cross defendant and moving party CBS Outdoor, Inc.

Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Denied. The challenged causes of action are not based on the cross-defendant's protected free speech or petitioning activity within the meaning of the anti-SLAPP statute. These causes of action raise only collateral or incidental facts with respect to any conduct falling within the applicable definition of the anti-SLAPP statutory scheme. See Wang v. Wal-mart Real Estate Business Trust (2007) 153 cal.app.4th 790. Defendant establishes the requisite probability of prevailing on the challenged causes of action. Request for attorney fees denied, motion not frivolous. Ms. Lee to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797

MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT MARK RUTHERFORD'S MOTION FOR LIMITED DISCOVERY PENDING MOTION TO STRIKE & DEMURRER. Appearances: Anthony Leones of Miller Starr Regalia (925.935.9400) appearing via Court Call for plaintiff and responding party; Cindy Lee of the Law Offices of Cindy Lee (885.5800) appearing via Court Call for defendant and moving party.
This matter was not argued. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Granted. Documents and verifications were not provided on the date they were due. The documents directly relate to the anti-SLAPP motion that was filed on a cross-complaint. Further, counsel stipulated to July 21, 2010 as the production date for documents and verifications. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797
